categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Quarterly earnings reports, revenue/EPS figures, profit margins, guidance, dividends, stock buybacks, and analyst reactions to reported financial performance.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Broad market roundups, sector rotation, macro data, index movements, and multi-stock previews where META is mentioned as part of a wider market or tech narrative.

categories.label: Analyst & Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst upgrades, downgrades, price target changes, buy/sell ratings, and institutional positioning commentary on META specifically.

categories.label: AI Strategy & Infrastructure
categories.description: Development and release of AI models (Llama), generative AI tools (Movie Gen, Meta AI assistant), AI chip deployment, data center buildout, energy deals, and open-source AI strategy.

categories.label: Regulatory, Antitrust & Compliance
categories.description: EU DMA/DSA proceedings, antitrust fines, competition probes, data privacy rulings, GDPR enforcement, and government regulatory actions targeting Meta's business practices.

categories.label: Child Safety & Youth Protection
categories.description: Lawsuits, legislation, Senate hearings, platform policy changes, and investigations related to minors' safety, teen accounts, content moderation for youth, and social media addiction.

categories.label: Hardware, AR/VR & Metaverse
categories.description: Quest headsets, Orion AR glasses, EssilorLuxottica partnership, Reality Labs products, mixed reality ecosystem, and hardware manufacturing strategy.

categories.label: Content Moderation & Geopolitics
categories.description: Banning RT, Turkey Instagram ban, election integrity, disinformation takedowns, political content decisions, Hamas-related content disputes, and country-specific censorship actions.

categories.label: Platform & Product Updates
categories.description: Feature launches across Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, Threads, and Messenger including creator tools, business features, advertising capabilities, music deals, and product integrations.

dimensions.scale: 1=trivial operational detail or routine corporate filing with no revenue/margin implications, 10=event directly and substantially alters revenue trajectory, cost structure, or total addressable market
1=outcome fully expected or confirms prior consensus, 10=outcome completely unexpected with no prior indication or dramatically divergent from expectations
1=transient event affecting only the next few days, 10=structural shift with implications lasting years
1=neutral or balanced tone with no clear positive/negative lean, 10=extremely strong positive or negative tone throughout the article
1=pure opinion, speculation, or vague commentary with no specific data, 10=rich in hard numbers, specific figures, dates, and verifiable facts
1=ambiguous impact that could reasonably be interpreted as positive or negative, 10=unambiguously and clearly good or bad for the company
1=affects only Meta specifically, 5=sector-wide implications for social media or tech, 10=macro or market-wide event
1=no regulatory, legal, or compliance dimension, 10=major legal ruling, regulatory fine, or compliance action with direct financial or operational consequences
1=no effect on Meta's competitive position, 10=fundamentally shifts competitive dynamics in a key market
1=reinforces the prevailing investor thesis about Meta, 10=fundamentally challenges or changes the investment narrative
1=rehash or follow-up of previously reported information with no new facts, 10=first report of entirely new information
1=no financial results reported or results exactly match expectations, 10=reported figures dramatically exceed or miss prior guidance and analyst expectations as described in the article
1=no indication of strategic direction or leadership intent, 10=major strategic pivot, new business line, or transformative leadership decision explicitly announced

kept.materiality: Ranges from Form 4 filings and routine blog posts about stickers to $1.4B lawsuit settlements and quarterly earnings revealing tripled EPS — wide variation across the corpus.
kept.surprise: Spans from routine scheduled product updates and expected regulatory actions to the first-ever dividend declaration and a 16% share price plunge on unexpected capex guidance — strong variation in how unexpected outcomes were.
kept.temporal_horizon: Ranges from transient service outages lasting hours to structural shifts like the EU DMA compliance model, open-source AI strategy, and multi-year EssilorLuxottica partnership that reshape Meta for years.
kept.sentiment_strength: Varies from neutral corporate announcements and feature updates to strongly negative articles about teen exploitation lawsuits and strongly positive articles about tripled earnings and first dividend.
kept.information_density: Ranges from one-line headline flashes with zero detail to full earnings call transcripts with specific revenue figures, EPS, margins, and forward guidance.
kept.directional_clarity: Some articles are clearly positive (earnings beat, dividend initiation) or negative (antitrust fines, lawsuit losses), while others like AI regulation or content moderation changes are genuinely ambiguous.
kept.scope: Ranges from Meta-only events like Orion glasses launch to sector-wide social media regulation bills and broad market selloffs affecting all tech.
kept.regulatory_risk: High variation — from zero regulatory angle in product feature posts to major EU antitrust charges, $1.4B Texas settlement, and multiple DMA/DSA proceedings.
kept.competitive_impact: Ranges from no competitive effect in routine updates to major shifts like open-sourcing Llama 3, launching Movie Gen to rival OpenAI, and the EssilorLuxottica AR partnership.
kept.narrative_shift: Most articles reinforce existing narratives (AI investment, regulatory pressure), but a few fundamentally shift the thesis — first-ever dividend signaling capital return era, Q1 guidance causing AI spending fears.
kept.repeatedness: Significant variation — many articles are duplicate reports of the same event (e.g., 5+ articles on EU DMA charges) while others break genuinely new information like the Texas settlement or Orion prototype.
kept.financial_result_surprise: Clear variation between articles with no financial data and earnings reports describing results that tripled EPS or guidance that caused 16% drops — this dimension is critical for the earnings category.
kept.strategic_signal: Ranges from no leadership angle in routine news to major strategic signals like Zuckerberg's open-source AI manifesto, Reality Labs restructuring, and the pivot to AI infrastructure spending.

dropped.expected_duration: Largely redundant with temporal_horizon in this corpus — articles that score high on one consistently score high on the other, providing little additional discriminating power.
dropped.actionability: Most Meta articles cluster at extremes — earnings and analyst actions are immediately actionable while regulatory and product articles are not — insufficient middle-range variation to be informative.
dropped.controversy: Most articles in this corpus reflect consensus views rather than divisive debates; even contentious topics like child safety regulation show broad agreement on the problem, just disagreement on solutions. Low variation across articles.
dropped.management_signal: Substantially overlaps with strategic_signal which better captures the variation observed; management-specific articles (Zuckerberg apology, AI team restructuring) are better scored through strategic_signal.

added.strategic_signal: Meta's corpus contains numerous articles where leadership explicitly signals strategic direction — Zuckerberg's open-source AI letter, AI team reorganization, Reality Labs investments, first dividend — that go beyond routine management commentary and warrant a dedicated dimension.
